stages.description: FASTQ-to-BAM alignment contract for governed BAM benchmark admission.

metrics.meaning: Mapped-read count derived from governed alignment summaries
Mapped fraction derived from governed alignment summaries

assumptions: optional read-two input is admitted only for paired-end alignment runs
reference index assets stay aligned with the selected aligner backend

stages.description: BAM authenticity stage contract.

metrics.meaning: Governed authenticity evidence score preserved across admitted BAM authenticity tools
Confidence assigned to the governed BAM authenticity evidence score
PMD-like authenticity signal retained in the governed BAM authenticity comparison surface
Upstream evidence inputs consumed by the governed BAM authenticity stage metrics contract
Expected authenticity evidence inputs that remained unavailable for the governed BAM authenticity benchmark row

assumptions: default stage assumptions captured

stages.description: BAM bias_mitigation stage contract.

metrics.meaning: Wall-clock runtime in seconds
Peak memory usage in MB

assumptions: admitted bias-mitigation tools must preserve the governed bias report, summary JSON, and benchmark-facing stage metrics

stages.description: BAM library-complexity contract for governed complexity-curve reporting, projected library-size estimation, and saturation benchmarking.

metrics.meaning: Projected unique-read yield preserved as the governed BAM complexity benchmark estimate when extrapolation is supported
Observed library saturation estimate preserved in the governed BAM complexity benchmark row when extrapolation is supported

assumptions: complexity extrapolation requires sufficient observed unique-read support before estimated_library_size and saturation_estimate can be emitted
the governed complexity_curve artifact is the benchmark-facing tabular curve even when the underlying backend is preseq

stages.description: BAM contamination stage contract.

metrics.meaning: Contamination scope preserved for the governed BAM contamination benchmark row
Whether the admitted BAM contamination tool satisfied the governed prerequisite checks
Contamination estimate preserved across admitted BAM contamination tools when prerequisites pass
Lower confidence interval bound preserved for the governed BAM contamination estimate
Upper confidence interval bound preserved for the governed BAM contamination estimate

assumptions: default stage assumptions captured

stages.description: BAM coverage stage contract with governed summary, depth sidecar, and benchmark-facing coverage metrics.

metrics.meaning: Mean observed depth across the governed target space
Fraction of governed target positions with at least one aligned base
Count of governed target positions with at least one aligned base
Count of governed target regions materialized in the BAM coverage comparison output
Governed ordered region identifiers preserved in the BAM coverage comparison output

assumptions: default stage assumptions captured
coverage summaries stay comparable only when target-space selection is fixed

stages.description: BAM damage stage contract with governed damage report, terminal-position metrics, parser-output JSON, and benchmark-facing damage signal semantics.

metrics.meaning: Observed 5' terminal C-to-T damage rate preserved in the governed BAM damage benchmark row
Observed 3' terminal G-to-A damage rate preserved in the governed BAM damage benchmark row
Governed categorical damage signal preserved in the BAM damage benchmark row
Wall-clock runtime in seconds
Peak memory usage in MB

assumptions: damage tools emit comparable terminal-position evidence from the same aligned BAM input
admitted and planned damage rows preserve governed damage-report, terminal-position-metrics, and parser-output semantics even when backend raw artifacts differ

stages.description: BAM duplication-metrics contract for governed duplicate-burden benchmarking, histogram reporting, and library-size estimation when available.

metrics.meaning: Observed duplicate-read count preserved in the governed BAM duplication-metrics benchmark row
Observed duplicate-read fraction preserved in the governed BAM duplication-metrics benchmark row
Estimated library size preserved in the governed BAM duplication-metrics benchmark row when the admitted tool can derive one

assumptions: duplicate-rate estimation requires coordinate-consistent BAM and read-group metadata
admitted BAM duplication-metrics tools must preserve the governed duplicate-count, duplicate-fraction, and estimated-library-size contract

stages.description: BAM endogenous-content stage contract with governed report JSON, summary JSON, stage metrics, and benchmark-facing mapped and endogenous read counts.

metrics.meaning: Observed total read count preserved in the governed BAM endogenous-content benchmark row
Observed mapped read count preserved in the governed BAM endogenous-content benchmark row
Estimated endogenous read count preserved in the governed BAM endogenous-content benchmark row
Estimated endogenous fraction preserved in the governed BAM endogenous-content benchmark row

Peak memory usage in MB

assumptions: endogenous ratio computed from stable mapping summary counts and host-contig rules
admitted endogenous-content tools must preserve governed total-read, mapped-read, endogenous-read, and endogenous-fraction semantics

Read count retained after the governed BAM filter policy is applied
Read count removed by the governed BAM filter policy
Named filter clauses that define the governed BAM filter benchmark row

assumptions: all admitted BAM filter tools must preserve the governed retained/removed read accounting contract

Observed AT-dropout percentage preserved in the governed BAM GC-bias benchmark row
Observed GC-dropout percentage preserved in the governed BAM GC-bias benchmark row

Peak memory usage in MB

Observed proper-pair count preserved in the governed BAM insert-size benchmark row

Governed pairwise kinship results carrying comparable relationship labels and concordance outputs

Observed corrected read-pair count preserved in the governed BAM overlap-correction benchmark row
Observed corrected overlap-base count preserved in the governed BAM overlap-correction benchmark row

metrics.meaning: Total reads observed in the input BAM
Reads reported as mapped in the governed flagstat summary
Reads reported as unmapped in the governed flagstat summary
Reads carrying the duplicate flag in the governed flagstat summary

defaults.rationale: Alignment baseline selected for broad BAM ecosystem compatibility.
Authenticity baseline chosen for clear operator interpretation.
Bias mitigation baseline preserves the admitted mapdamage2 projection surface.
Complexity baseline preserves the governed preseq extrapolation surface.
Contamination default is established for mtDNA-focused checks.
Coverage default offers stable low-overhead reporting.
Damage baseline preserves comparability with historical aDNA runs.
Duplication metrics baseline favors deterministic duplicate summaries.
Endogenous estimate baseline derives from deterministic mapping summaries.
Filtering baseline minimizes contract variance across workflows.
GC-bias baseline preserves the single admitted Picard reporting surface.
Genotyping baseline preserves the governed low-depth ANGSD execution surface.
Haplogroup baseline preserves deterministic assignment through the admitted Yleaf surface.
Insert-size baseline preserves deterministic paired-template QC summaries.
Kinship baseline prioritized for stable pairwise outputs.
Length filtering baseline emphasizes transparent minimum-length provenance.
Mapping summary baseline uses deterministic samtools summaries.
MAPQ filtering baseline uses ubiquitous deterministic filtering behavior.
Duplicate marking baseline preserves deterministic BAM audit artifacts and repeatable burden summaries.
Overlap correction baseline preserves deterministic overlap clipping and before/after audit artifacts.
Early BAM sanity checks stay grounded in deterministic samtools count summaries.
Recalibration baseline preserves the governed BQSR execution surface.
Sex inference baseline is lightweight and deterministic.
Validation baseline favors deterministic diagnostics and ubiquity.

deprecations.rationale: Example policy entry used to enforce deprecation-window contract checks.

stages.description: Prepare immutable contaminant depletion databases for FASTQ reference-screening stages.

metrics.meaning: Number of contaminant source sequences admitted to the governed database build.

assumptions: contaminant database comparability requires source bundle identity, build backend, and index checksum provenance.

stages.description: Prepare immutable rRNA screening databases for FASTQ depletion and QC stages.

metrics.meaning: Number of rRNA source sequences admitted to the governed database build.

assumptions: rRNA database comparability requires source collection, build command, index digest, and taxonomy scope provenance.

stages.description: Prepare immutable taxonomy classifier databases for FASTQ screening stages.

metrics.meaning: Number of source taxonomy records admitted to the governed database build.

assumptions: taxonomy database comparability requires source accession, taxonomy dump, build command, and checksum provenance.

stages.description: Capture a pre-execution provenance snapshot for governed FASTQ plans.

metrics.meaning: Number of plan, tool, asset, and input entries captured in the provenance snapshot.

assumptions: plan certification requires a provenance snapshot that binds stage graph, tools, containers, runtime assets, and input checksums.

stages.description: Classify FASTQ input layout before route-family-specific planning.

metrics.meaning: Confidence score for inferred single-end, paired-end, interleaved, or merged-read layout.

assumptions: layout classification must distinguish independent single-end, paired-end, interleaved, and premerged inputs before safety rules are applied.

stages.description: FASTQ amplicon stage for deterministic OTU clustering and representative sequence emission.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads contributing to OTU clustering.
Canonical benchmark alias for the governed OTU identity threshold.
Number of OTU clusters emitted.
Number of samples represented in the governed OTU abundance table.
Number of representative OTU sequences emitted.
Canonical benchmark alias for the representative OTU sequence count emitted with the OTU table.
Stage runtime in seconds
Peak memory usage in MB

assumptions: OTU identity threshold is declared in the run configuration.
OTU clustering uses reproducible parameters and deterministic centroid ordering.
paired-end libraries must be merged or otherwise reduced to one analysis stream before OTU clustering.

stages.description: Concatenate lane-split FASTQ inputs with governed sample and lane provenance.

metrics.meaning: Number of input lanes concatenated under governed ordering.

assumptions: lane concatenation requires stable lane ordering and explicit sample identity preservation.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for error correction of reads.

metrics.meaning: Number of reads emitted through the corrected FASTQ outputs
Number of reads whose sequence or quality changed during correction
Number of reads preserved byte-equivalent across correction outputs
Number of input reads
Number of corrected reads
Number of input bases
Number of corrected bases
Number of input read pairs
Number of output read pairs
Rate of k-mer fixes applied
Mean base quality before correction
Mean base quality after correction

assumptions: single-end input omits reads_r2 and corrected_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization across corrected outputs.
error-correction backends may emit tool-native working files, but the governed adapter normalizes corrected FASTQ outputs before downstream handoff.

stages.description: Convert governed interleaved FASTQ inputs back to paired mate files.

metrics.meaning: Number of paired reads recovered from interleaved FASTQ layout.

assumptions: deinterleaving is layout-only and must preserve pair identity and order.

stages.description: Split multiplexed FASTQ inputs into sample FASTQs using governed barcode manifests.

metrics.meaning: Number of reads assigned to governed sample barcodes.

assumptions: demultiplexed scientific outputs require fixed barcode kit identity and mismatch policy.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for host read depletion via reference mapping.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads.
Number of reads after host depletion.
Canonical benchmark alias for reads removed during host depletion.
Number of input bases.
Number of output bases after host depletion.
Number of input read pairs.
Number of output read pairs after host depletion.
Fraction of input reads removed during host depletion.
Canonical benchmark alias for fraction of input reads classified as host.
Structured governed depletion summary for host depletion.

assumptions: reference index must identify the governed host build used for depletion.
single-end input omits reads_r2, host_depleted_reads_r2, and removed_host_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization.
removed_host_reads_* artifacts carry the reads classified as host by the depletion aligner.
host_depletion_report_json is the canonical governed depletion report; backend-native mapping metrics remain traceable from that report.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for contaminant/decoy screen distinct from taxonomy classification.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads.
Reads retained after contaminant depletion.
Canonical benchmark alias for reads removed during contaminant depletion.
Number of input bases.
Number of retained bases after contaminant depletion.
Number of input read pairs.
Number of retained read pairs after contaminant depletion.
Fraction of reads removed during reference contaminant depletion.
Canonical benchmark alias for fraction of reads removed as reference contaminants.
Structured governed depletion summary for contaminant depletion.

assumptions: contaminant decoy reference is explicitly versioned and separate from taxonomy DB.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and contaminant_screened_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization during contaminant depletion.
removed_contaminant_reads_* artifacts carry the reads rejected by contaminant depletion while preserving mate synchronization.
contaminant_screen_report_json is the canonical governed depletion report; backend-native alignment metrics remain traceable from that report.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for optional rRNA depletion/screening.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads
Reads retained after rRNA filtering
Canonical benchmark alias for retained reads after rRNA depletion
Reads removed as rRNA during depletion
Canonical benchmark alias for reads removed as rRNA during depletion
Number of input bases
Number of retained bases after rRNA filtering
Number of input read pairs
Number of retained read pairs after rRNA filtering
Fraction of input reads removed as rRNA
Canonical benchmark alias for fraction of reads removed as rRNA
Structured governed depletion summary for rRNA depletion

assumptions: rRNA reference bank is versioned separately from host and taxonomy databases.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and rrna_filtered_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization.

stages.description: Inspect likely adapter content without mutating reads prior to trimming, while emitting governed adapter-report and identity evidence.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads inspected for adapter evidence.
Number of reads preserved after governed adapter inspection.
Number of input bases inspected for adapter evidence.
Number of bases preserved after governed adapter inspection.
Mean base quality observed across the inspected reads.
Canonical adapter-inspection report path emitted for governed benchmark collection.
Number of governed adapter candidates detected in the evidence report
Governed adapter-bank identifiers admitted by adapter inspection after stronger parent motifs suppress nested partial seeds.
Highest governed per-adapter support fraction among the admitted adapter identities.
Minimum governed support fraction required to admit an adapter identity for this report.
Fraction of reads with detectable adapter evidence when the backend can estimate it

assumptions: single-end input omits reads_r2; paired-end input contributes both mates to governed evidence-only adapter inspection.
fastqc inspects the full input set, so sampled-read evidence is not claimed by the canonical contract.
governed benchmark rows must expose adapter_report, detected_adapter_ids, detection_confidence, and detection_threshold fields from the canonical adapter-inspection report.

stages.description: Report duplicate-like signals before merge only when route-family evidence justifies the interpretation.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads inspected by the report-only premerge duplicate detector.
Number of reads flagged by the report-only premerge duplicate detector.
Fraction of input reads flagged by the report-only premerge duplicate detector.
Number of read-pair observations inspected when paired-end input is present.
Resolved report-only duplicate-detection policy recorded by the governed internal harness.
Measurement scope used to interpret premerge duplicate-like sequence signatures.

assumptions: premerge duplicate detection is not equivalent to aligned duplicate marking and must stay report-only until route evidence is present.

stages.description: Detect sequencer-family artifacts before trimming and route-family safety decisions.

metrics.meaning: Number of governed instrument-artifact signals detected in FASTQ quality or sequence patterns.

assumptions: instrument-artifact detection is report-only until a governed route explicitly consumes the artifact class.

stages.description: Estimate FASTQ library complexity before alignment-dependent duplicate marking is available.

metrics.meaning: Number of reads inspected by the governed prealignment library-complexity estimator.
Canonical prealignment complexity estimate exposed for comparison rows when input data is sufficient.
Prealignment estimate of library duplication or redundancy.
Deterministic reason emitted when the governed estimator cannot produce a comparable complexity estimate.

assumptions: prealignment complexity estimates are exploratory unless benchmarked against aligned duplicate metrics.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for extracting UMIs and propagating them into read headers.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads
Number of reads after UMI extraction and tag propagation
Number of input bases
Number of output bases
Number of input read pairs
Number of output read pairs after UMI extraction
Number of reads for which a UMI was extracted and attached.
Number of reads for which governed UMI extraction did not yield a valid UMI tag.
Canonical number of reads emitted with extracted UMI tags.
Canonical number of reads that remained untagged because UMI extraction failed.
Canonical FASTQ header tag format used to propagate extracted UMIs.

assumptions: UMI extraction must preserve read order and pair synchronization.
UMI barcode pattern must match the library design for emitted tags to remain meaningful.
canonical report_json semantics remain comparable across governed UMI extraction runs.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for removing low-complexity reads.

metrics.meaning: Canonical filtered FASTQ output for read one or single-end input.
Canonical filtered FASTQ output for read two when paired-end input is provided.
Number of input reads.
Number of output reads.
Number of input bases before low-complexity filtering.
Number of output bases after low-complexity filtering.

Mean base quality after low-complexity filtering.
Derived read and base retention deltas for low-complexity filtering.
Reads removed due to low complexity filters.

assumptions: low-complexity filtering must remain deterministic for identical input.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and filtered_fastq_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization during filtering.
canonical report_json semantics remain comparable across supported low-complexity backends.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for filtering reads.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads
Number of output reads
Number of reads removed by filtering
Reads removed due to N content

Reads removed due to low complexity filter
Reads removed due to k-mer contaminant filtering
Reads removed due to contaminant k-mer filtering
Reads removed due to length thresholds

Number of output bases
Number of input read pairs
Number of output read pairs
Mean base quality before filtering
Mean base quality after filtering
Derived retention and quality deltas

assumptions: single-end input omits reads_r2 and filtered_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization during filtering.

stages.description: Prepare mapper-ready reference indexes for FASTQ reference-guided stages.

metrics.meaning: Canonical benchmark alias for the emitted index directory consumed by downstream reference-guided stages.
Structured benchmark list of emitted index files and their sizes.
Canonical benchmark alias for governed index build runtime in seconds.
Canonical benchmark alias for total bytes written for the emitted reference index.
Total bytes in the indexed reference FASTA input
Number of emitted reference index files
Total bytes written for the reference index

assumptions: selected index backend must match the downstream mapper or host-depletion backend that consumes the index.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for amplicon denoising and ASV table generation.

metrics.meaning: Number of inferred ASVs
Number of samples represented in the ASV table
Number of representative sequences emitted alongside the ASV abundance table.

assumptions: asv inference applies only to amplicon libraries with denoising-aware governance.
single-end input omits reads_r2; paired-end denoising retains mate-aware error modeling through the governed dada2 runtime.

stages.description: Convert paired FASTQ mates into governed interleaved FASTQ layout.

metrics.meaning: Number of paired reads written to interleaved FASTQ layout.

assumptions: interleaving is layout-only and must preserve pair identity and order.

stages.description: Materialize a governed QC manifest that binds FASTQ reports, inputs, tools, and asset identities.

metrics.meaning: Number of FASTQ QC reports represented in the governed manifest.

assumptions: QC manifest materialization must preserve report-level checksums and tool identities before aggregation.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for merging paired-end reads into merged amplicon or overlapping read fragments.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads observed across both mates before merging
Number of reads emitted after merge and unmerged handling
Number of input bases observed across both mates before merging
Number of output bases emitted after merge and unmerged handling
Number of input read pairs eligible for merging
Number of output read pairs represented after merge processing
Number of forward reads
Number of reverse reads
Canonical number of input read pairs eligible for merging
Number of successfully merged pairs
Number of pairs left unmerged
Canonical number of merged read pairs emitted by the benchmark row
Canonical number of unmerged read pairs retained by the benchmark row
Canonical number of input read pairs discarded after merge handling
Fraction of input pairs that merged

assumptions: merge suitability depends on paired-end overlap and library model.
governed report_json is the canonical merge contract and may preserve the backend-native merge log as raw_backend_report_txt for provenance.

stages.description: FASTQ-derived amplicon stage for normalizing feature-table abundance values.

metrics.meaning: Canonical benchmark alias for the governed abundance-normalization method.
Number of rows in the normalized abundance table
Number of samples in the normalized abundance table
Number of distinct features represented in the normalized abundance table.
Per-sample normalized abundance totals used to validate the compositional output contract.
Whether every governed sample total matches the expected compositional target for the selected normalization method.
Fraction of normalized cells equal to zero

assumptions: input abundance table is produced by an upstream feature-inference or clustering stage.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for primer-aware amplicon orientation normalization.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads presented for primer normalization.
Number of reads emitted after primer normalization.
Number of reads whose configured primer sequence was matched and trimmed during normalization.
Number of input reads that did not match the governed primer set during normalization.
Number of primer-derived bases removed while normalizing read orientation and primer boundaries.
Fraction of reads that required primer trimming
Fraction of reads normalized to forward orientation

assumptions: primer normalization applies only to amplicon libraries with explicit primer governance.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and normalized_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization.

stages.description: Normalize FASTQ read names when downstream tools require a stable header convention.

metrics.meaning: Number of read headers modified by governed normalization.

assumptions: read-name normalization is unsafe for provenance-sensitive workflows unless original headers are recoverable.

stages.description: Prepare immutable adapter banks for adapter detection and trimming stages.

metrics.meaning: Number of adapter records admitted to the governed bank.

assumptions: adapter-bank comparability requires fixed sequence records, orientation policy, and checksum provenance.

stages.description: Prepare immutable host reference bundles for host-associated FASTQ depletion routes.

metrics.meaning: Number of governed files emitted for the host reference bundle.

assumptions: host depletion comparability requires a fixed host build, alias set, and index-producing backend.

stages.description: Prepare immutable primer banks for primer normalization and amplicon route planning.

metrics.meaning: Number of primer records admitted to the governed bank.

assumptions: primer-bank comparability requires marker regime, orientation policy, mismatch policy, and checksum provenance.

stages.description: FASTQ report-only stage for profiling overrepresented sequence content.

metrics.meaning: Number of profiled overrepresented sequences
Number of overrepresented sequences that crossed the governed reporting threshold.
Fraction carried by the dominant overrepresented sequence

assumptions: overrepresented sequence profiles are diagnostic and do not mutate reads.
single-end input omits reads_r2; paired-end input contributes both mates to sequence ranking.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for read-length profiling.

metrics.meaning: Number of reads observed.
Minimum read length observed before trimming.
Average read length before trimming.
Median read length before trimming.
Maximum read length observed in the governed profile.
Number of distinct read lengths observed in the governed profile.

assumptions: report_json is the governed read-length report contract and the canonical comparison surface for this stage.
length histogram generation must be deterministic for identical reads.
single-end input omits reads_r2; paired-end input contributes both mates to the histogram.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for read profiling without mutation.

metrics.meaning: Total reads observed
Total bases observed
Mean base quality
GC percentage
Read length histogram

assumptions: single-end input omits reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate-level summaries.
qc_json is the governed read-profile report contract and remains the canonical comparison surface for profile_reads.
qc_tsv preserves the seqkit tabular backend summary, while qc_plots_dir contains normalized histogram tables derived from the governed report.

stages.description: FASTQ amplicon stage for chimera detection and governed representative-sequence retention reporting.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads.
Number of non-chimeric output reads.
Canonical benchmark alias for the retained non-chimeric representative-sequence count.
Number of reads removed as chimeric.
Canonical benchmark alias for the representative-sequence count removed as chimeric.
Fraction of input reads flagged as chimeric.

assumptions: Reference or de-novo chimera detection mode is fixed per run.
Chimera reference database id/version/checksum is explicit when reference mode is used.
paired-end libraries must be merged or otherwise reduced to one analysis stream before chimera removal.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for duplicate removal while preserving pair semantics.

metrics.meaning: Resolved tool identifier for duplicate-removal observation.
Resolved worker threads executed by the selected duplicate-removal backend.
Governed duplicate-definition mode applied by the selected backend.
Whether duplicate removal ran on single-end or paired-end reads.
Whether the governed plan preserves input read order in emitted deduplicated reads.
Number of input reads.
Canonical benchmark alias for the number of input reads.
Number of output reads.
Canonical benchmark alias for the number of reads removed as duplicates.
Canonical benchmark alias for the number of deduplicated reads retained.
Canonical benchmark alias for the number of output reads.
Number of reads removed as duplicates.
Fraction of reads removed as duplicates.
Whether paired duplicate removal preserved the governed pair count contract.
Number of duplicate classes preserved in the governed duplicate-class summary.
Structured governed duplicate provenance summary emitted by the backend.
Native backend report format preserved alongside the governed duplicate summary.

assumptions: paired-end input may emit r1+r2 outputs; single-end may omit r2 artifact.
report_json is the canonical governed deduplication contract; duplicate_classes_tsv and duplicate_provenance_json preserve class-level and backend-lineage evidence for comparison.

stages.description: Repair mate synchronization for paired FASTQ inputs before pair-sensitive stages.

metrics.meaning: Number of reads that could not be reconciled into synchronized pairs.

assumptions: pair repair changes read ordering and must publish orphan accounting before benchmark comparison.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for MultiQC aggregation of FASTQ quality-control reports.

metrics.meaning: Aggregation backend selected for governed QC reporting
Governed QC aggregation scope used to select upstream contributors
Number of input reads
Number of output reads (post-QC reference)
Number of input bases
Number of output bases (post-QC reference)
Number of input read pairs
Number of output read pairs
Mean base quality
Estimated contamination rate
Maximum observed adapter-content burden across governed QC contributors
Mean adapter-content burden across governed QC contributors
Duplicate-rate summary carried into the governed QC aggregation report
Ambiguous-base rate summary carried into the governed QC aggregation report
Count of governed k-mer warning events represented in the QC aggregation report
Count of governed overrepresented-sequence findings represented in the QC aggregation report
Number of governed QC artifacts aggregated into the canonical report
Distinct governed contributor stage identifiers
Distinct governed contributor tool identifiers
Stable governed QC lineage fingerprint for the aggregated inputs
Number of samples represented in the MultiQC report
Number of modules represented in the MultiQC report
Path to raw FastQC output directory
Path to trimmed FastQC output directory
Path to MultiQC HTML report
Path to MultiQC data directory

assumptions: the stage aggregates existing FASTQ QC inputs rather than recomputing primary read statistics.
governed QC artifacts may come from read profiling, adapter inspection, taxonomy screening, validation, or downstream cleanup stages that emit governed QC summaries.
report_json is the canonical governed aggregation contract; multiqc_report and multiqc_data remain backend-native deliverables for human review and downstream inspection.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for read-level taxonomic screening with governed classifier, database, and top-taxa provenance.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads
Number of output reads (screening does not remove reads)
Number of input bases
Number of output bases (screening does not remove bases)
Number of input read pairs
Number of output read pairs
Fraction of reads classified as contaminants
Governed classifier backend selected for taxonomy screening
Governed format emitted for taxonomy screening summaries
Catalog identifier of the taxonomy database used for screening
Artifact identifier of the taxonomy database used for screening
Canonical benchmark alias for the governed taxonomy database artifact identifier.
Minimum classification confidence threshold enforced by governed screening
Whether unclassified reads were retained in governed taxonomy outputs
Structured governed taxonomy summary entries reduced from the raw classifier report
Canonical benchmark read count derived from the governed classified fraction and input read total.
Canonical benchmark read count derived from the governed unclassified fraction and input read total.
Fraction of reads assigned to non-unclassified taxa under the governed taxonomy report
Fraction of reads remaining unclassified under the governed taxonomy report
Highest-abundance classified taxa preserved under the governed taxonomy report contract

assumptions: supported tools must emit read-level taxonomic classifications from the screened FASTQ input.
screen_report_tsv preserves the raw classifier summary while classification_report_json stores the governed taxonomy report contract.
when the selected backend supports unclassified-read export and emit_unclassified is enabled, unclassified_reads_* preserve the reads that remained taxonomically unresolved.

stages.description: Subsample FASTQ inputs with deterministic seeds for fair benchmark cohorts.

metrics.meaning: Number of reads retained by governed subsampling.

assumptions: benchmark subsampling requires published seed, sample fraction, and input checksum provenance.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for trimming instrument-derived polyG tails with governed backend-report normalization.

metrics.meaning: Resolved trim-polyg worker threads executed by the selected backend.
Whether the stage ran on single-end or paired-end reads.
Whether terminal polyG trimming was enabled for the run.
Minimum terminal polyG run length required before trimming is applied.
Number of input reads.
Number of output reads after polyG trimming.
Number of input bases before polyG trimming.
Number of output bases after polyG trimming.
Number of input read pairs observed before polyG trimming.
Number of output read pairs emitted after polyG trimming.
Mean base quality before polyG trimming.
Mean base quality after polyG trimming.
Derived read/base retention and quality deltas for polyG trimming.
Number of reads whose terminal polyG tail was trimmed.
Number of bases removed from polyG tails.
Resolved polyX bank identifier used to govern polyG trimming chemistry defaults.
Native backend report format preserved inside the governed trim-polyg report.
Resolved polyX bank hash used to record chemistry provenance for the run.
Resolved polyX bank preset used to govern trim-polyg chemistry defaults.

assumptions: polyG trimming remains a no-op on inputs without terminal G-tail artifacts.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and trimmed_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization during terminal trimming.
governed report_json is the canonical trim-polyg contract and preserves backend-native report provenance for downstream comparison.
backend-native reports are emitted as optional raw_backend_report_json or raw_backend_report_txt artifacts when the selected backend supports them.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for trimming reads.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads
Number of output reads
Number of input bases
Number of output bases
Number of reads removed by trimming or post-trim retention rules
Number of bases removed between input and trimmed output
Number of input read pairs
Number of output read pairs
Mean base quality before trimming
Mean base quality after trimming
Derived retention and quality deltas
Whether the executed trim run operated on single-end or paired-end reads
Resolved adapter handling policy executed by the selected trim backend
Resolved polyX handling policy executed by the selected trim backend
Resolved ambiguous-base handling policy executed by the selected trim backend
Resolved contaminant handoff or trimming policy executed by the selected trim backend
Backend-native report format preserved alongside the governed trim report when available
Adapter bank id used for trimming
Checksum of adapter bank used for trimming
Adapter preset name used for trimming
Adapter enable/disable overrides used for trimming

assumptions: adapter, polyx, and contaminant trimming policies are explicit in effective params.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and trimmed_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization during trimming.
report_json is the governed trim-stage report; backend-native reports are preserved separately when the selected tool can emit them.
unsupported policy and layout combinations must fail during trim planning instead of silently degrading execution.

stages.description: Damage-aware FASTQ pretrim for aDNA libraries with explicit terminal masking/trimming policies.

metrics.meaning: Resolved 5' trim length applied during damage-aware trimming.
Resolved 3' trim length applied during damage-aware trimming.
Number of input reads.
Number of reads after damage-aware pretrim.
Number of reads retained after damage-aware pretrim.
Number of input bases before damage-aware trimming.
Number of output bases after damage-aware trimming.
Number of bases removed by damage-aware terminal trimming.
Number of input read pairs observed before damage-aware trimming.
Number of output read pairs emitted after damage-aware trimming.
Mean base quality before damage-aware trimming.
Mean base quality after damage-aware trimming.
Resolved damage trimming mode used by the governed execution policy.
Resolved execution policy applied to terminal damage handling.
Requested 5' trim length recorded by governed terminal-damage planning.
Requested 3' trim length recorded by governed terminal-damage planning.
UDG handling classification used for terminal damage policy.
Terminal C>T / G>A asymmetry before trimming.
Terminal C>T / G>A asymmetry after trimming.
Derived read/base retention and quality deltas after damage-aware trimming.

assumptions: UDG classification is provided via config/env or inferred deterministically from sample metadata.
execution_policy defaults to policy_derived, which preserves UDG-trimmed ends only for the governed udg_trimmed default trim profile and otherwise applies explicit terminal trimming.
Terminal masking/trimming policy is explicit and reproducible.
Refusal occurs when downstream aligner requires untrimmed reads.
single-end input omits reads_r2 and trimmed_reads_r2; paired-end input preserves mate synchronization during terminal trimming.

stages.description: FASTQ stage for structural validation and format-integrity accounting before downstream planning.

metrics.meaning: Number of input reads
Number of reads carried forward by this report-only validation stage
Number of input bases
Number of bases carried forward by this report-only validation stage
Number of input read pairs
Number of read pairs carried forward by this report-only validation stage
Total number of reads observed
Number of reads that passed validation
Number of reads that failed validation
Mean Phred quality score
Number of FASTQ inputs examined by the governed validator.
Number of read pairs examined by the governed validator when paired input is present.
Whether pair-synchronization checks were executed for this validation run.
Whether pair-synchronization checks passed when pair synchronization was evaluated.
Whether paired-end read counts matched between mates under governed validation.
Whether strict validation policy criteria passed for this run.
Governed validation failure classification summarizing the dominant validation issue class.

assumptions: validation is report-only and may not modify FASTQ payloads
pair_sync_policy defaults to require_header_sync for paired inputs and not_applicable for single-end inputs during effective-parameter resolution
validated_reads_manifest records governed lineage for downstream graph provenance without duplicating FASTQ payloads
benchmark comparisons require identical input hashes, strict-mode policy, and governed validation artifacts regardless of backend-specific native outputs

stages.description: Verify runtime asset locks and checksums before FASTQ execution starts.

metrics.meaning: Number of locked runtime assets verified before execution.

assumptions: asset verification must fail closed for world-class-closed plans when any declared asset lock is missing or mismatched.

defaults.rationale: Stable OTU cluster policy with reproducible identifiers.
Default corrector chosen for robust error-correction baseline.
Default host depletion uses deterministic mapping and unmapped extraction.
Default contaminant screen uses reference-guided depletion against governed decoy references.
Default rRNA filter is established for pre-HPC screening.
Default adapter detector remains report-only and avoids mutating reads.
Governed internal contract harness records report-only duplicate-signal expectations from the owned paired-end corpus without treating the result as scientific duplicate removal.
Governed internal contract harness records prealignment library-complexity report expectations without substituting for alignment-derived metrics.
Default UMI handler extracts barcodes without dropping reads.
Default complexity filtering uses deterministic entropy/polyX thresholds.
Default filter preserves compatibility with downstream metrics.
Default FASTQ reference preparation must emit the mapper index consumed by governed host and reference-contaminant depletion.
Governed ASV inference now uses the admitted containerized dada2 backend and publishes the canonical infer-asvs report plus taxonomy-ready representative sequences.
Default merger is stable for paired-end overlap handling.
Abundance-table normalization stays within the admitted amplicon table tooling set.
Deterministic primer trimming with explicit mismatch/orientation controls.
Default overrepresented-sequence detector uses FastQC module output.
Default length histogram baseline provides deterministic pre-trim size distribution.
Neutral stats baseline is lightweight and deterministic.
Deterministic UCHIME-based chimera screening remains the baseline before broader ensemble admission.
Default deduplication uses pair-aware duplicate removal with deterministic mate preservation.
QC aggregation default provides operator-first summary output.
Default classifier maximizes comparability with existing reports.
Default polyG trimmer uses fastp polyG-tail handling for common sequencer artifacts.
Baseline trimmer for deterministic quality/throughput tradeoff.
Damage-aware pretrim default uses deterministic terminal mask/trim policy for aDNA damage-aware pretrim.
Validation default has broad FASTQ format coverage.

stages.description: VCF stage contract for vcf.admixture in the governed domain registry.

cluster_assignments_sum_to_one_within_tolerance_per_sample

stages.description: VCF stage contract for vcf.call in the governed domain registry.
assumptions: diploid_calling

stages.description: VCF stage contract for vcf.call_diploid in the governed domain registry.
assumptions: Diploid genotype model is valid for the cohort.

stages.description: VCF stage contract for vcf.call_gl in the governed domain registry.
assumptions: Likelihood-based calling is used for low-coverage contexts.
GL caller defaults are locked (calling model, mapQ/baseQ, and depth filters).
GL calling emits provenance with parameter values and source references.

stages.description: VCF stage contract for vcf.call_pseudohaploid in the governed domain registry.
assumptions: Pseudohaploid random-allele or consensus model is fixed and documented.
Pseudohaploid sampling policy is deterministic via explicit seeded configuration.
Sampling seed and policy are logged in output artifacts.

stages.description: VCF stage contract for vcf.damage_filter in the governed domain registry.
assumptions: C>T/G>A and PMD thresholds are explicit and versioned.
Damage filtering must support PMD-based filtering and C>T/G>A masking strategies.
Bias audit section must summarize before/after damage filtering metrics.

stages.description: VCF stage contract for vcf.demography in the governed domain registry.

generation_time_and_recombination_model_are_fixed_per_run

stages.description: VCF stage contract for vcf.filter in the governed domain registry.
assumptions: pass_filtering

stages.description: VCF stage contract for vcf.gl_propagation in the governed domain registry.
assumptions: GL/PL tags are retained through filtering and post-processing.
Propagation checks must assert GL fields survive into imputation-ready inputs.

stages.description: VCF stage contract for vcf.ibd in the governed domain registry.
assumptions: phasing_quality_thresholds_are_fixed_per_run
ibd_outputs_emit_metrics_json_with_schema_bijux.vcf.ibd.v1

defaults.rationale: deterministic baseline caller for current production profile.
deterministic diploid baseline for the current governed production profile.
current governed production default keeps GL emission runnable while `angsd` remains a planned low-coverage alternative.
current governed production default preserves the pseudohaploid contract while `angsd` remains a planned alternative.
deterministic PMD/C>T-G>A masking contract anchor for the current governed profile.
stable filtering semantics for regression comparability.
preserves GL fields across downstream handoffs in the current governed profile.
keep the governed imputation-metrics contract anchored to the same backend family that produces the source imputation evidence.
current governed production default keeps explicit panel-aware imputation runnable while retained alternatives stay comparative.

current governed PCA default keeps sample-complete eigenvector output, eigenvalue reporting, and metadata joins anchored to the PLINK2 smoke-backed cohort path while retained `eigensoft` stays comparative.
current governed production default keeps phasing anchored to the dedicated phasing backend while Beagle and Eagle stay comparative.
the governed population-structure baseline keeps consumed PCA and admixture evidence on the admitted PLINK-family structure path with explicit sample-group distance summaries.
deterministic normalization/filter baseline for governed post-imputation outputs.
deterministic reference panel prep baseline.
the governed cohort-QC baseline keeps missingness, MAF, and HWE summaries anchored to the admitted PLINK-family execution surface while retaining deterministic bcftools compatibility for comparison.
minimal required metrics for quality gating.
